name: Warm Apple Spice Cake
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 cups green apples, such as Granny Smith or Pippin, chopped, unpeeled
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------------CARAMEL SAUCE-------------------------------
1/3 cup granulated sugar
1 cup light-brown sugar (packed)
1/4 cup maple syrup
1/4 cup dark corn syrup
1 cup whipping cream
--------------------------------FOR SERVING--------------------------------
Vanilla ice cream. In a mixing bowl, sift flours, baking soda, nutmeg, cinnamon, mace, ginger, and salt. Set aside. Blend oil and sugar in an electric mixer at medium speed for 5 minutes. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition. Gradually fold the dry ingredients into the oil mixture, combining thoroughly. Add apples, pecans, and raisins, blending well with a spatula. Pour batter into a buttered and floured 8-inch springform pan. Bake at 325 degrees for 1 1/2 to 2 hours, or until a knife inserted comes out clean. Serve slices of cake drizzled with caramel sauce and topped with one scoop of vanilla ice cream per slice. Makes 10 to 12 servings.
CARAMEL SAUCE: In a heavy-bottomed saucepan, mix granulated sugar, brown sugar, maple syrup, corn syrup, and whipping cream. Cook over high heat until the mixture reaches 200 degrees on a candy thermometer. Let cool for 20 minutes. Skim the surface if necessary.
